title: DBT Skills Practice Plan
description: Use this checklist to build and maintain your personal DBT skills practice. Like any skill, DBT skills get stronger with regular practice — not just during crises, but as part of daily life. Review this plan weekly to track what you're practicing and where you need to focus.
instructions: Check off the items you have in place or are actively practicing. For unchecked items, consider making them a priority this week. Come back regularly to update your progress.

items.label: I practice a Wise Mind check-in at least once daily
I practice observing and describing my emotions without judgment
I practice doing one thing at a time with full attention (one-mindfully)
I have TIPP supplies accessible (ice packs, cold water, space for movement)
I have a written crisis plan with specific skills to use at different distress levels
I practice radical acceptance for things I cannot change
I label my emotions specifically (not just 'bad' or 'upset') at least once daily
I check the facts before acting on strong emotions
I use opposite action when my emotion doesn't fit the facts or acting on it would be harmful
I maintain ABC PLEASE basics: accumulate positives, build mastery, cope ahead, treat PhysicaL illness, balanced Eating, avoid mood-Altering substances, balanced Sleep, Exercise
I use DEAR MAN when I need to ask for something or say no
I use GIVE skills to maintain important relationships
I use FAST skills to maintain my self-respect in interactions
I track my emotions and skill use in a daily log
I review my skills practice weekly and identify areas to strengthen
I have professional support (therapist, DBT group, or counselor) or know how to access it

disclaimer: This practice plan supports ongoing DBT skills development. It is not a substitute for professional DBT therapy, which includes individual therapy, skills group, phone coaching, and therapist consultation team. If you are struggling with emotional dysregulation, self-harm, or suicidal thoughts, please seek professional help. Call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) for immediate support.
